A GUIDE TO CONTEXT ENGINEERING FOR LLMS
articles.summary: Studies show that feeding more tokens into an LLM's context window negatively impacts performance, with one 2025 Chroma study showing that accuracy drops from 95% to 60% as input size grows. This challenge is addressed by “context engineering,” which strategically manages the model's input through four tactics: using external memory, selecting relevant information via RAG, compressing conversation history, and isolating concerns across multiple agents.

articles.title: BUILDING A RELIABLE AND EXTENDABLE NOTIFICATIONS PLATFORM
articles.summary: Monday.com addressed its monolithic Rails notification system, which was hard to extend and suffered from rate-limiting losses, by migrating it to a TypeScript microservice with a simple JSON and SDK interface. This new system uses a three-stage SQS flow (process, filter, and deliver) to manage 8M+ daily notifications across platforms like email, mobile, and Slack.

articles.title: THE CULT OF VIBE CODING IS INSANE
articles.summary: Fully vibe coding, where developers avoid inspecting source code, leads to unnecessary technical debt and poor software quality. AI is most effective when humans actively guide it through detailed review and problem-solving rather than treating the code as a black box.

articles.title: WHAT BEING RIPPED OFF FOR $35K TAUGHT ME
articles.summary: A consultant hired for a Beijing augmented reality project was defrauded of $35,000 after working tirelessly for an inexperienced and resistant team. The ordeal provided painful lessons on the unreliability of contracts and the necessity of trusting one's professional instincts when dealing with difficult clients.

articles.title: WORKSHOP: A PRACTICAL GUIDE TO OBSERVABILITY CONSOLIDATION
articles.summary: Most observability stacks aren't designed, they accumulate and fight over your time and budget. In this workshop [10], leaders at Sentry and Recurly will provide a practical framework for consolidating your stack. Learn how to evaluate options, identify tradeoffs, and use AI

articles.title: to connect everything in a single system. Register here
DEFUDDLE
articles.summary: Defuddle is an open-source tool that extracts core content from web pages by stripping away clutter like sidebars and ads into clean HTML or Markdown. It has consistent output and extensive metadata extraction across browsers, Node.js, and CLI environments.

articles.title: HIPPO MEMORY
articles.summary: Hippo-memory is a biologically-inspired memory system that prevents AI agents from forgetting context by mimicking the human hippocampus through mechanisms like memory decay and consolidation. It has hybrid search and multi-tool management to create more adaptive agents that intelligently retain only the most relevant information.

articles.title: DOES GITHUB STILL MERIT “TOP GIT PLATFORM FOR AI-NATIVE DEVELOPMENT” STATUS?
articles.summary: GitHub's availability has drastically dropped to 90% due to infrastructure overload from a massive increase in AI coding agent traffic, leading to frequent outages. These issues are caused by saturated systems like databases and Redis clusters, alongside problematic failover mechanisms that struggle with the higher demand.

articles.title: SAM ALTMAN MAY CONTROL OUR FUTURE—CAN HE BE TRUSTED?
articles.summary: An investigation into OpenAI CEO Sam Altman showed persistent doubts about his trustworthiness and suitability to steer AI development, prompting his brief 2023 firing based on internal memos alleging deceptions about safety protocols. Despite a career pattern of prioritizing personal power over stated ethical commitments, Altman was reinstated due to overwhelming pressure, cementing his control over OpenAI while fundamental questions about his integrity persist.

articles.title: MOBILE OBSERVABILITY: IT'S ABOUT TIME (AND LATENCY)
articles.summary: Join Luciq on a new event series exploring how agentic AI is redefining what it means to truly observe, understand, and act on mobile app performance. Through candid conversations, real customer stories, you'll understand what modern mobile observability looks like

articles.title: and how your team benefits. SIGN UP HERE
[MODEL] CLAUDE CODE IS UNUSABLE FOR COMPLEX ENGINEERING TASKS WITH THE FEB UPDATES (GITHUB THREAD)
articles.summary: Claude Code's ability to perform complex engineering tasks has severely degraded since February, a decline directly attributed by quantitative analysis to the reduction and redaction of its "structurally required extended thinking" tokens.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC EXPANDS PARTNERSHIP WITH GOOGLE AND BROADCOM FOR MULTIPLE GIGAWATTS OF NEXT-GENERATION COMPUTE
articles.summary: Anthropic has expanded its compute partnership with Google and Broadcom to meet growing Claude AI demand, as it has reached a $30B annual run rate of revenue.

articles.title: THE DCO DEBATE: WHO IS RESPONSIBLE FOR AI-GENERATED CODE?
articles.summary: The open-source community, through a Node.js debate and wider consensus, affirms that AI-assisted code is compatible with the Developer Certificate of Origin (DCO) as long as a human contributor takes full responsibility for reviewing, understanding, and disclosing the code.

articles.title: GHOST PEPPER
articles.summary: Ghost Pepper is a 100% local, hold-to-talk speech-to-text application for macOS that transcribes and pastes spoken input directly on your machine using on-device AI models.
